title: Xeridic Yeast-Glazed Mediterranean Bellflower-root-doraji Wrap
description: A yeasty, flaky, date-night mediterranean dish featuring bellflower-root-doraji, glaze, ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly, wrapped in a ti-leaf. Perfect for a romantic evening.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix yeast with 1 cup of warm water (around 10062F). Stir gently to dissolve, then let sit for 5 minutes, or until frothy.
Add yeast mixture to a mixing bowl with 2 cups of flour, 1 teaspoon of paprika, and 104 teaspoon of salt. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead dough for 10 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle to medium-high heat (around 40062F).
Divide dough into 4 equal pieces, and roll each into a ball. Flatten each ball slightly into a disk shape.
Place a disk of dough on the skillet, and brush with olive oil. Top with sliced bellflower root-doraji, a sprinkle of paprika, and a pinch of salt.
Fold dough over filling, and press edges to seal. Cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
While cooking, mix date honey with 1 tablespoon of water in a small bowl until smooth. Brush glaze over cooked wraps during the last minute of cooking.
Repeat with remaining dough and filling ingredients.
To assemble wraps, place a cooked disk on a ti-leaf, and fill with remaining bellflower root-doraji, a sprinkle of paprika, and a pinch of salt.
Brush edges of ti-leaf with olive oil, and fold to enclose.
Serve immediately, garnished with fresh herbs or edible flowers, if desired.
